title: Networking And Firewall Configuration
description: Practice RHCSA v9 hostname management, hostname resolution, persistent routes, service networking, and firewall access control.

tasks: Set the persistent hostname of clientvm to clientvm.lab.example.com.
Add a persistent hostname-resolution entry so registry.lab.example.com resolves to 192.168.122.3.
Add a persistent static route for 192.168.50.0/24 via 192.168.122.3 on the active non-NAT connection.
Configure httpd to listen on port 8080 and serve a simple page from /var/www/html/index.html.
Open the firewall for TCP port 8080 and verify the service is reachable locally.
solution_commands: CONN=$(nmcli -t -f NAME,IP4.ADDRESS connection show --active | awk -F: '$2 ~ /^192\.168\.122\./ {print $1; exit}')
hints: Use hostnamectl for the hostname task.
Use a persistent local hostname-resolution method that survives reboot.
Use nmcli to inspect the active private-network connection before changing routes.
For the web task, adjust both the service configuration and the firewall.
solution_outline: Add a static hostname-resolution entry for registry.lab.example.com.
Apply the route via nmcli so it survives reboot.
Change Apache to listen on 8080, start it, and open the port in firewalld.

tasks: Add a persistent hostname-resolution entry for registry.lab.example.com to 192.168.122.3.
Add a persistent route for 192.168.50.0/24 via 192.168.122.3.
Configure httpd to listen on port 8080 and serve /var/www/html/index.html.
Open firewall access for TCP port 8080 and verify the local curl succeeds.
solution_commands: CONN=$(nmcli -t -f NAME,IP4.ADDRESS connection show --active | awk -F: '$2 ~ /^192\.168\.122\./ {print $1; exit}')
